extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out implies not sent) holds on all reachable executions. Reasoning: sent is set true ONLY by t2 (send_attempt under guard `not opted_out`), so whenever sent becomes true, opted_out is false at that step (antecedent false, property satisfied). opted_out is set true ONLY by t1 (optout), which simultaneously assigns sent:=false, so immediately after opted_out becomes true, sent is false. Once opted_out=true, no transition ever resets it to false (no update sets opted_out:=false). And while opted_out=true, every applicable event forces sent false: t1 (optout) sets sent:=false, t3 (send_attempt under opted_out) sets sent:=false, t4 (tick) sets sent:=false. The only sent:=true setter (t2) is disabled because its guard `not opted_out` is false. Therefore no reachable state can have opted_out=true and sent=true; the invariant is inductive and holds for all executions (including up to 14 events). No counterexample exists.
